chains.name: Tech

signals.note: Slightly above average, within normal range

signals.source: USPTO patent filings by tracked companies (24h)

chains.conclusion: Only one RISING signal in the Tech chain today: Hacker News stories matching keywords — autonomous driving, LiDAR perception, protein folding, and foundation model biology — surged 25.5% above the 30-day average, suggesting a spike in community discussion around these topics. arXiv submissions are essentially flat. USPTO data is silent, removing a key patent-activity signal. Crunchbase funding is slightly below average. With only one RISING signal, this is noise-level and does not constitute a trend flag.

chains.name: Economy

signals.source: Sector layoff news items — tracked industries (24h Google News count)
Sector job posting volume — Adzuna (sum across categories)

chains.conclusion: The Economy chain shows one RISING signal and one DROPPING signal, netting to noise-level convergence. Layoff news items surged 80% above the 30-day average — 45 Tech layoff items and 38 AI-sector items dominate, with Pharma contributing 3 and Finance 8, suggesting broad-based workforce reductions concentrated in technology and AI. Job posting volume is 13.2% below average, approaching but not breaching the DROPPING threshold. The yield spread is flat and uninformative. The cardboard box index is silent. The layoff spike is the most material signal and warrants monitoring.

chains.name: Biotech

signals.source: NIH Reporter active grants this FY
signals.note: 13.7% above 30d average — borderline but NIH grants at 13181 vs 11590 avg

chains.conclusion: NIH Reporter active grants reached 13,181 this fiscal year versus a 30-day average of 11,590 — a 13.7% increase that falls just short of the 20% RISING threshold. Keywords monitored include CRISPR, mRNA, CAR-T, PROTAC, and epigenetic reprogramming, suggesting capital is flowing into gene editing and next-generation therapeutics. bioRxiv preprints are modestly elevated. ClinicalTrials enrollment is flat at zero new starts. S-1 filings are below average. With only one signal technically RISING, convergence is insufficient to flag a trend, though NIH grant momentum is worth watching.

chains.name: Social

signals.source: Google Trends avg interest score (7d window)
Amazon Movers and Shakers item count

chains.conclusion: The Social chain shows one severe DROPPING signal: Google Trends interest in autonomous vehicle, robotaxi, protein folding, and small modular reactor collapsed to 2.0 from a 30-day average of 23.1 — a 91% decline. This is a dramatic drop in public search interest across all monitored keywords simultaneously, which may reflect a data anomaly or a genuine pullback in consumer-facing attention. Bluesky is silent. Kickstarter and Amazon signals are flat. No RISING signals exist; convergence is zero and this chain is noise.

chains.name: Geopolitics

signals.source: % of tracked commodities above own 30d baseline

chains.conclusion: The Geopolitics chain has one severe DROPPING signal: the percentage of tracked commodities — lithium, copper, wheat, crude oil, gold — trading above their 30-day baseline collapsed to 60 from an average of 503, indicating broad commodity price weakness. This is a significant deterioration across the commodity complex and may reflect demand destruction fears, dollar strength, or unwinding of geopolitical risk premiums. All other geopolitics signals — shipping congestion, EU consultations, Congressional hearings — are flat at zero. No RISING signals; convergence is zero.

chains.name: Corporate

signals.source: SEC Form 4 insider filings for watchlist (24h)
Keyword-matched job postings across watchlist companies (total)
USPTO patent filings by watchlist companies (24h)

chains.conclusion: The Corporate chain shows no RISING signals today. Form 4 insider filings are below average at 1 versus 1.5, suggesting no unusual insider buying activity across NVIDIA, Google, Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly, Moderna, WeRide, and other watchlist names. Job postings are flat. USPTO patent data is silent. 8-K filings at 3 versus a 3.9 average are modestly below trend. Secondary signals show notable options activity in GOOGL (C/P +28%), MRNA (+88%), ALTO (+261%), ZS (+164%), PATH (+74%), and BEAM (+85%), but these are secondary signals without a chain-level RISING trigger to anchor them.

chains.name: Energy

signals.source: Average energy commodity price (lithium, uranium, copper)

chains.conclusion: The Energy chain has one RISING signal: Crunchbase energy funding news items came in at 1 versus a 30-day average of 0.6 — a 67% increase, suggesting a pickup in venture and growth capital deployment into energy-related companies. ARPA-E data is silent, removing a key government R&D signal. arXiv applied physics preprints and average commodity prices for lithium, uranium, and copper are both flat. With only one RISING signal, this is noise-level and does not constitute a trend flag, though the funding uptick is worth monitoring.
